page: Microsoft Azure M32ts is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the MS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 38 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, supporting hardware flags such as AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA.

The server is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.0 GB of RAM per core. Although designated as a "Tiny Memory" configuration within its family, it provides a substantial memory allocation alongside 1049 GB of local storage. The instance is also Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance external disk attachments. It does not include any GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear division between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, with Geekbench and stress-ng single-core tests placing in the bottom 10% to 25% tier. However, multi-core execution scales effectively to average levels, as demonstrated by Passmark CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression benchmarks. Memory bandwidth remains consistently in the average tier for both cached small-block and uncached large-block read/write operations.

This hardware profile is suited for workloads that benefit from multi-threaded processing and moderate memory capacity, such as database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference. Qualitatively, the M32ts offers a cost-effective entry point into Azure's memory-optimized class by providing dedicated Xeon resources and local storage without the premium associated with larger memory configurations.
description: Microsoft Azure M32ts is an x86_64 server in the MS family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, 1049 GB of local storage, and is Premium Storage capable, though it lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal weak single-core performance, but multi-core capabilities, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform at average levels. This configuration is suited for multi-threaded workloads, database hosting, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference. It provides a balanced, qualitative cost-efficiency profile for users requiring dedicated Intel Xeon resources and moderate memory capacity without the higher expense of larger memory-optimized instances.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M32ts is an x86_64 server featuring 32 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1049 GB local storage. It delivers average multi-core and database performance for multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M32ts is an x86_64 server with 32 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1049 GB local storage, suited for database and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core processor platform with balanced memory and local storage for database operations and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
192.0 GB of system memory and 1049 GB of local storage
Premium Storage capable for high-performance disk attachments
Average multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Suited for database hosting, static web serving, and multi-threaded applications
